title: On the Waves of the Night
subtitle: Jesus comes walking on the sea and lifts Peter from drowning

stillness: Be still, and let the night come down.
There is a darkness in which the Lord seems absent.
Wait for Him as the watchman waits for the morning.
gospel: The Lord was praying alone upon the mountain.
The little boat, with the disciples in it, was tossed in the midst of the sea by the waves.
They labored at the oars, for the wind was against them.
And in the fourth watch of the night He came to them, walking upon the sea.
When the disciples saw Him walking on the sea, they were troubled with fear,
But at once He spoke to them: Take heart; it is I; do not be afraid.
Then Peter said: Lord, if it is You, bid me come to You upon the waters.
And Peter went down out of the boat and walked upon the water to come to Jesus.
But seeing the wind strong and violent, he was afraid, and began to sink, and cried out.
And immediately the right hand of the Lord raised him up, that he should not drown,
and said to him: O you of little faith, why did you doubt?
behold: Attend to each thing, as if you were present.
Behold the disciples, and have compassion on them,
for they are in great anguish and tribulation:
the tempest has fallen upon them, the night is upon them, and they are without their Lord.
wearied with long watching and lengthy prayer, alone, in the night, with naked feet,
He comes down from the toilsome and perhaps stony mountain — and have deep compassion on Him.
Behold how He walks upon the sea with firm step, His feet not sinking, as though upon dry land;
for the creature knew its Creator.
And see Peter, set ablaze and goaded by a vehement love,
leaping into the sea, forgetting for the love of Christ the peril of the water.
consider: See how the Lord did not stand by at the beginning of the danger, but at its end.
He permitted His disciples to be imperiled, that they might become patient,
that they should not look for help at the very beginning of their troubles.
He lets us be in the midst of perils, that we may run to Him alone,
who alone is able to deliver us when we least hope for it.
For when the human mind can no longer provide for itself, then the divine salvation draws near.
Peter's faith was burning; but human weakness dragged him down into the deep.
Upon the sea the Lord makes him walk, to show the power of His divinity;
He lets him sink, that he may know his own weakness,
lest he think himself equal to God, and grow proud.
He is left a little while to the temptation, that through prayer his faith may be increased.
Neither could the wind have harmed him, if his faith had been firm.
If to Peter, who had so confidently asked, it is said: O you of little faith, why did you doubt? —
what shall be said of us, who have not even the least part of his little faith?
prayer: deign to climb into the little boat of my heart,
and still the windy gusts of pride and the storms of rising vices,
that no wind of temptation may overturn me, nor any wave drown me.
Free me from the tempests of this stormy sea,
and lead me to the tranquillity of the quiet shore,
granting me now the peace of time and of heart,
and after this, the peace of eternity. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Fides ardebat; sed humana infirmitas in profundum trahebat.
latinJewel.english: His faith was burning; but human weakness was dragging him down into the deep.
